_marker: CONTRADICTION REVIEW QUEUE — computed truth vs static bindings. Resolution is HUMAN-ACK ONLY: extract/computed/resolve.ts applies computed-wins per explicitly named item; nothing auto-resolves.

items.cause: cross-component zeroing (the showcase's own named Checkbox gap, now measured): Polaris's Choice wrapper CSS zeroes the checkbox backdrop border and repaints the control edge as an inset box-shadow, so the carried Checkbox.module.css border-width binding loses the cascade — computed border-width is 0. Computed truth wins: resolve via the review queue (--to {p.border-width-0}; several zero-valued tokens share 0px, border-width-0 is the exact name-class match). The edge itself rides the minted box-shadow channel.
